title: Guarianthe aurantiaca
taxonomy: Guarianthe aurantiaca is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the yellow hibiscus. It is native to Cuba and is a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Guarianthe aurantiaca is a species of hibiscus that is part of the Guarianthe genus. The plant is classified as a flowering perennial and is closely related to other species of hibiscus in the same genus. Guarianthe aurantiaca is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta.
uses: Guarianthe aurantiaca, also known as the yellow hibiscus, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's flowers and leaves are used in various herbal remedies, particularly in Latin American folk medicine. Some studies suggest that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its medicinal uses.
